operation: First, initialize a workplane aligned to the XZ coordinate plane, sketch a circle with a 15 unit radius on this workplane, then extrude the sketch 60 units along the Y axis to form a right circular cylinder. Next, transform the active workplane by rotating it 90 degrees around the X axis and offsetting it 25.5 units along the Z axis, split the cylinder with this workplane, and retain only the solid portion above the split plane. Finally, translate the resulting cut solid 30 units along the positive Y axis to get the final model.
geometry: This is a uniform prismatic solid with a consistent cross-section along its long axis. The cross-section perpendicular to the length direction is a circular segment, with a straight flat chord at the top and a smooth circular arc at the bottom. The part has a single flat rectangular top face spanning its full length, a continuous curved cylindrical underside, and two matching planar segment-shaped end faces, with no extra cutouts or protruding features.
function: This is a prismatic functional component with a flat mounting top surface and curved lower contact surface, suitable for use as a sliding guide, wear strip, support shim, or conveyor interface part where a stable mounting face and curved contact interface are required.
